sync_type: Unidirectional (HubSpot → Mailchimp) via native connector; Bi-directional via middleware (Zapier/n8n/Make)
popularity: very_high — most common email-CRM integration pair

authentication_requirements.hubspot_side: OAuth 2.0 with content scopes. For native integration: connect via HubSpot Marketplace app.
authentication_requirements.mailchimp_side: API Key with admin-level access. OAuth 2.0 for middleware integrations (access token never expires).

common_use_cases: Automatically add HubSpot form submissions to Mailchimp audience
Sync contact name and email from HubSpot to Mailchimp for email campaigns
Track Mailchimp email engagement (opens, clicks) back in HubSpot
Tag Mailchimp subscribers based on HubSpot lifecycle stage or deal stage
Sync unsubscribe status bidirectionally to comply with CAN-SPAM
Segment Mailchimp audiences based on HubSpot custom properties

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: HubSpot property format doesn't match Mailchimp merge tag format (e.g., phone with country code in a numeric-only field). Mailchimp silently drops the value.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Pre-create merge fields in Mailchimp matching HubSpot property names. Validate and sanitize data formats before pushing. Use middleware with field transformation logic.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Using POST /members to add a contact that already exists. POST is create-only.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Always use PUT /members/{subscriber_hash} for upsert (idempotent create/update). The subscriber_hash is the MD5 hash of the lowercase email address.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Native integration is one-way (HubSpot → Mailchimp only). When a contact unsubscribes in Mailchimp, HubSpot doesn't know and keeps emailing.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Implement Mailchimp webhooks for 'unsubscribe' events. Create a custom HubSpot property 'mailchimp_unsubscribed'. Set it to true when webhook fires. Use HubSpot workflow to prevent re-emailing.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Exceeded 10 simultaneous connections during large batch sync.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Implement request queuing (max 10 concurrent). Use Mailchimp Batch endpoint for operations >10K records.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Exceeded 110 requests per 10 seconds or daily limit during sync.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use HubSpot Operations Hub for managed sync. Implement webhooks instead of polling. Reduce sync frequency.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: HubSpot free/starter plans don't support custom field mappings in native Mailchimp integration.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Upgrade to Marketing Hub Professional for custom mapping. Or use middleware (Zapier, n8n) to handle the field mapping externally.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Native integration maps one HubSpot list to one Mailchimp audience. Cannot route contacts to different audiences based on lifecycle stage or deal property.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use a middleware workflow layer (n8n, Zapier, Make) that evaluates contact records and routes conditionally to different Mailchimp audiences.

community_solutions.problem: Contacts created before integration installed don't sync
community_solutions.solution: Export existing contacts from HubSpot and import into Mailchimp manually (or via API bulk import) before enabling the integration. The native integration only syncs contacts created after installation.

community_solutions.problem: Duplicate audience members with overlapping audiences
community_solutions.solution: Consolidate to one audience using Tags for segmentation. Use the subscriber_hash as unique identifier and check for existing members before adding.

community_solutions.problem: Mailchimp merge field character limits cause silent data loss
community_solutions.solution: Add pre-sync validation in middleware: truncate fields to Mailchimp limits, normalize formats (phone, date). Map only the fields you need, not all HubSpot properties.
